title: Realtime Collaborative Editor
subtitle: Google Docs-scale conflict resolution using CRDTs in the browser
description: A fully browser-based collaborative text editor built on Yjs CRDTs and WebRTC peer signaling. Supports concurrent multi-cursor editing with sub-50ms sync latency across LAN peers. Implements a custom undo/redo stack that respects concurrent edit history.

technicalChallenges.title: CRDT Merge Conflicts Under High Concurrency
technicalChallenges.description: Standard OT algorithms fail under simultaneous three-way edits. Yjs Y.Text with CRDT semantics resolves all merge cases deterministically without a central authority server, enabling true peer-to-peer convergence.

technicalChallenges.title: WebRTC Signaling Without a Dedicated Server
technicalChallenges.description: Used y-webrtc's built-in BroadcastChannel fallback for same-device tabs and a lightweight Cloudflare Worker as the ICE signaling relay, keeping infrastructure costs at zero for the demo tier.

assets.imageAlt: Screenshot of collaborative editor with two cursors visible in a shared document
